frame_analyses.description: Right needle driver grasping needle, left grasper holding suture. Needle positioned appropriately in needle driver jaws
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good needle grasp position, suture clearly visible

frame_analyses.description: Left grasper holding pink Penrose drain, right needle driver approaching with needle for first mark penetration
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Drain properly stabilized, needle approaching at appropriate angle

frame_analyses.description: Needle passed through first mark on drain, suture being pulled through
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean needle passage, good suture control

frame_analyses.description: Preparing for second mark penetration, drain repositioned for access
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Smooth repositioning of drain, maintaining suture tension

frame_analyses.description: Both suture ends visible, beginning extracorporeal knot formation outside the trainer
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Adequate suture length for knot tying, both ends accessible

frame_analyses.description: Knot pusher being used to advance tied knot down to tissue surface
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper knot pusher technique, knot appears well-formed

frame_analyses.description: Text overlay shows 'Slide and lock' - knot being secured at drain surface
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Final knot positioning and locking phase

frame_analyses.description: Task completed - drain visible with suture in place across both marks
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Suture appears properly positioned, drain intact

penalties.description: Minor deviation from mark positions - estimated 1-2mm total

confidence_rationale: Task completion time clearly observable. Drain integrity and knot placement well-visualized. Suture deviation estimates conservative due to camera angle and mark visibility limitations.
cannot_determine: Exact millimeter measurements of suture deviation from marks due to camera angle and mark visibility
Precise knot pusher technique details during extracorporeal phase
technique_summary: Good intermediate-level performance with efficient needle loading and knot pushing phases. Extracorporeal knot formation took longer than expert benchmark but was executed correctly.
improvement_suggestions: Practice extracorporeal knot formation to reduce from 50s to under 30s
Focus on more precise suture placement relative to marks
